reasoning: The question requires Shakhriyar Mamedyarov to gain 1% of his current 2742 Elo rating, which equals almost exactly 28 points (reaching 2770+). For a super-GM with a K-factor of 10, gaining 28 points in a single month requires scoring 2.8 points above expectation (e.g., scoring +5 or +6 against peers), reflecting a consistent ~2850+ performance level. This is exceptionally rare, supported by the empirical base rate of 0.007 for such jumps over typical forecasting horizons. Additionally, FIDE lists only update monthly on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on the first resolution date (2025-11-16), Wikipedia will almost certainly still display the November 1 rating of 2742, making the probability near 0. For the second date (2025-12-09), this covers just one active month, so the probability remains at the lowest allowable estimate. By the final date (2026-02-07), three FIDE updates will have occurred, increasing the mathematical possibility slightly, but a +28 point surge from a veteran currently trending below his peak still warrants a very low assigned probability. I scale uncertainty slightly upward for distant bounds.
